text.title: Two-Layer Secret Scanning Failure Modes
text.summary: Three antipatterns observed when deploying two-layer secret scanning. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Developer commits with `--no-verify` to ship a fix; secret enters local git history; CI catches it 30 min later.
antipattern.root-cause: Hook is slow or noisy; team trained to bypass.
antipattern.fix: Tune gitleaks to staged-files-only; CI mirrors the gitleaks scan as a required check so `--no-verify` is caught at PR time.

antipattern.symptom: `gitleaks.toml` has 30 allowlist entries; nobody remembers why they're there.
antipattern.root-cause: Entries added without comments; never re-audited.
antipattern.fix: Require a one-line comment per allowlist entry (rule + reason); quarterly re-audit removes entries that no longer apply.

antipattern.symptom: CI reports verified AWS token; PR merged with the comment "we'll rotate later"; token used by attacker within 24h.
antipattern.root-cause: No automated paging on verified findings; rotation not enforced before merge.
antipattern.fix: Make verified-finding a hard CI failure; auto-page security owner; block merge until rotation evidence (e.g., AWS STS GetCallerIdentity returns AccessDenied on the old key) is attached.
